RRSlRTt RWR RR RR’ W4 ---- 5. arezra KRI -nfad T(R —7R7R 6. 3RRBJ &KI "tiRifl 'Jllffl/ 3t3*iP,fl ^Rlfif Ril RR MSRM — R^TR e«l«i 30?qq, ^+4 yfaq P^IPH m/4/131 /09-3RfT. ] -- 3 of 8 -- 4 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [Part III—Sec. 4] NATIONAL COUNCIL FOR TEACHER EDUCATION NOTIFICATION . . New Delhi, the 6th May, 2009 No. F. 9-34/2007/NCTE/Estt.—Regulations laying down Recruitment Rules for appointment of various levels in National Council for Teacher Education were notified on 27th March, 2003 vide National Council for Teacher Education (Recruitment) Regulations, 2003, Some amendments in the Recruitment Regulations (RRs) relating to the post of Under Secretary/Research Officer (US/RO) were under consideration for quite some time and were approved by the Council in its meeting held on 12-12-2008. Subsequently, the proposed amendment in RRs to the post ofUS/RO has been approved by the Mihistty ofHRD vide their letter No.26-49/2007/EE-10, dated 27-03-2009 with a direction to notify the modified RRs. ' Now in exercise of the powers conferred under Section 10 (2) read with Section 32(2) (c) of National Council for Teacher Education Act, 1993 and with die approval of the Central Government, the National Council for Teacher Education hereby makes the following Recruitment (Second Amendment) Regulations relating to the post of Under Secretary/Research Officer (US/RO). 1. Short title and commencement 1.1 These Regulations may be called the National Council for Teacher Education (Recruitment) (Second Amendment) Regulations, 2009. „ 12 They shall come into force from the date of its notification in Gazette of India. . 2. Amended Recruitment Regulations to the post of Under Secretary/Research Officer are attached as Appendix-1 to this Notification. 3. Supersession The Recruitment Regulations for the post of Under Secretary/ Research Officer notified in the Gazette Notification No. 36 F. No. 9-12/2002/NCTE, dated the 27th March, 2003 stands superseded w.e.f. the date ofpublication of this notification. 4. Other general conditions as stipulated in the Notification dated 27th March, 2003 remain unaltered. Chairperson—NCT —Chairman N.A Section Officer/Programme Officer and Private 2. Vice-Chairperson, NCTE —Member Secretary in NCTE with 8 years of regular 3. Member Secretary —Member service in the grade. 4. One Member from Ministry —Member Note 1 —The eligibility list for promotion shall of HRD be prepared with reference to the date of comple- 5. One Expert to be nominated —Member tion by the officers of the prescribed qualifying service in the respective grade/post. 6 Note 2.—Where juniors who have completed their qualifying service are being consi dered for promotion, their seniors would also have to be considered provided they are not short of qualifying/eligibility service by more than half the qualifying/eligibility service or two years, whichever is less, and have success fully completed their period of probation for promotion to next higher grade along with their juniors who have completed such qualifying/eligibility service. Deputation (including short-term contract): Officers under the Central/State Governments/ Universities/Recognised Research Institutions/ Public Sector Undertakings/Statutory, Semi Government Autonomous or other organisations :— (a)(i) holding analogous posts on regular basis in the parent cadre/department; or (ii) with 5 years’ regular service in the grade rendered after appointment thereto on regular basis in the Pay Band 3 i.e.Rs. 15,600—39,100with by Chairperson Member belonging to SC/ST—Member to be nominated by Chairperson -- 5 of 8 -- 6 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [Part III—Sec. 4] _______________ TO______________ . Grade Pay of Rs, 5400 (Pre-revised scale Rs. . 8,000-13,500) or equivalent in the parent cadre/ department; or , • (iii) with 8 years’ regular service in the grade rendered after appointment thereto on regular basis in the Pay Band 2 i.e. Rs. 9300—34800 with - . Grade Pay of Rs. 4200 (Pre-revised scale Rs. 6500-10500) or equivalent in the parent cadre; department; and (b) Possessing the following qualifications, experience (i) Experience in educational administration in various Directorates of Education, SCRET, KVS, NVS or such schoo 1/educational systems etc.; and (ii) Knowledge in use of information and communication technology such as handling data-base management system and use of off line and on-line electronic resources. [Period of deputation (including short-term contract) including period of deputation (including short-term contract) in another ex-cadre post held immediately preceding this appointment in the same or some other Organisation/ Department of the Central/State Government shall not ordinarily exceed 3 years which cap be extended maximum up to 5 years with the-, concurrence of the lending organization. The maximum age-limit for appointment by deputation (including short-term contract) shall be not exceeding 56 years, as on the closing date of the receipt of applications.] HASIB AHMAD, Member Secy. Tfafax xffarr Trsffm (RARfi, RTfas afa faqifafa) fafadti, 2007 4fi wtt 5 (5) 2009-2010 < fax fafa7 4R4IM4. fasjr 4144*4 RFWaFptfcT TRT=T fax fax jffa ^417 3FJStR Tfatfatl 4$ t : •qit^^l4lfai4i 7T4 3T8?fx.2009-2010 4» fax $4fa Tlfafcfat'4> 71R H=t>l4l TX *J°f 3tfaXH'-1fat 7T tfal faffafaf 4> HiqqilT 4» 3EJRK sfa *lclsbHl;j«k st>4 4ft «l’iix 7^ qufai^ 4>t 'jiix'H 44T Hi-qej UXfH fax 3TR4T WH fax '3fa TfaRra i?nt 2009 w ^fatt 4R ffar ^rxnr i 1 “ ’ inrfa aflTHX, wi ttfag ■ [fagnnm/4/3rai./i3i/09j NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 13th May, 2009 F.No. 48-3(l)2008ZNCTE (N&S).—Regulations laying down the procedure for grant of recognition along with Norms and Standards for various teacher training courses were promulgated by the National Council for Teacher Education viJeNCTE (Recognition Norms and Procedure) Regulations, 2007, dated 27th November, 2007 notified on 10th December, 2007, The matter regarding cutoff date prescribed in the clause 5(5) of these Regulations has further been reconsidered and it was decided to amend the cut off date for grant of recognition/permission to teacher education institutions for the current academic session i,e, 2009-2010. Accordingly, in modification of the above Regulations and in exercise of the powers conferred under Section 32(2) of the NCTE Act, 1993, the NCTE hereby makes the following Regulations: 1. Short title and commencement (1) These Regulations may be called the National Council for Teacher Education (Recognition Norms and Procedure) (SecondAmendment)Regulations,2009, , (2) They shall come into force w.e.f. the date oftheir publication in the Official Gazette. 2. Definitions In these regulations, unless the context otherwise requires, all the words and expressions used herein and defined in the National Council for Teacher Education Act, 1993 (73 of 1993) shall have the same meaning respectively assigned to them in the said Act. 3. Applicability These regulations shall be applicable to all matters relating to teacher education programmes covering norms, standards and procedures for recognition of institutions, commencement of new programmes and addition to sanctioned intake in existing programmes and other matters incidental thereto. These amended regulations shall be applicable only for grant ofrecognition/permission to start teacher-training course for the current academic session i.e, 2009-2010 and not for any subsequent year. From the next academic session i,e. 2010-2011, the cut off date mentioned in clause 5(5) of the Regulations shall strictly be enforced. -- 7 of 8 -- 8 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [Part HI—Sec. 4] 4. Extent of Amendment Clause 5(5) of the NCTE (Recognition Norms and Procedure) Regulations, 2007, is modified as under only for grant of recognition/permission for starting various teacher training courses for current academic session i.e. 2009-2010. All complete applications pending with the Regional Committees shall be processed for the current academic session i.e. 2009-2010 in accordance with the provisions of relevant Regulations and maintaining the chronological sequence and final decision, either recognition granted or refused, shall be communicated by 31 st July, 2009.
